blocks.content: Ten snowballs for ten silver? That's one silver each? That's pretty expensive.
Having grown up in the mountains and never experienced festivals, Shirone found it culturally shocking.
But Amy took it as a matter of course.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Tourist attractions always have a premium. Besides, that Ice Queen plush doll is quite famous. It's strategically made by the autonomous district and can only be obtained during the winter festival. The types of dolls change every year, so there's even a collection.
Oh, I see.
Shirone's eyes sparkled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it's a festival limited edition, it's natural for the price to increase over time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he could win the game, paying ten silver for ten snowballs wouldn't be a bad deal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright, I'll give it a try. Watch me.
Shirone paid ten silver.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ten snowballs, please.

blocks.speaker: Announcer
blocks.content: Here we have another brave participant. May the blessing of the Ice Queen be with you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The blessing of the Ice Queen didn't seem like a good thing, but since her character was a tourist attraction, he let it slide.
Shirone received the box of snowballs.
Entering the game area, he saw someone else attempting the challenge.
While waiting, he picked up a snowball from the box and found it was mixed with water, making it hard and heavy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, this should have a decent range.
Throwing it wasn't the problem.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: However, he noticed that the snowball had to be thrown through a window in the wall to reach the opposite tower.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The throwing angle was restricted, making it difficult to generate power and significantly reducing accuracy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whew, this is harder than I thought.
The previous challenger wiped his sweat and stepped back after failing.
People who visited the winter festival every year knew how difficult it was to win the Ice Queen collection, so they weren't disappointed.
Following the announcer's instructions, Shirone entered the game area, set the box down, and picked up a snowball.
He rolled it between his fingers to gauge the distance, then opened his stance and threw the snowball with all his might.
The snowball passed through the window, fell due to gravity, and shattered against the castle wall below the watchtower.
The tourists laughed cheerfully.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Normally, Shirone would have laughed it off too, but with Amy watching, his face turned red.
He didn't dare look back at her and quickly picked up the next snowball, analyzing his failure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can't increase the angle, so I can't generate power. This is much harder than I thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Grr...!
Shirone gritted his teeth and threw the snowball.
He barely managed to get the distance right, but the snowball missed the soldier target and fell elsewhere.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whew! Whew!
Determined, Shirone threw the snowballs one after another.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Having succeeded in Thunder Strike in one go, he thought he could eventually succeed by reducing the margin of error bit by bit.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But after throwing five snowballs, he realized.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is harder than Thunder Strike.
The rules were simple, but it was not a game made haphazardly.
It was designed to look easy to succeed, but the throwing angle and distance were cleverly adjusted so that only those with natural strength or professional skills could succeed.
After throwing ten snowballs, Shirone was panting.
Despite the cold weather, his body was hot, and sweat formed on his forehead.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He couldn't take off his coat because he was wearing Armand.
Shirone sheepishly stuck out his tongue and returned to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh, sorry. I failed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: There's nothing to be sorry about. It's just a game. But that was really hard.
Through Shirone's challenge, Amy guessed that the game's difficulty was top-notch.
Analyzing with her red eyes, she calculated that there weren't many routes to hit the target considering the force, gravity, and air resistance.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Haha! You failed spectacularly. And you're supposed to be a magic school student. How embarrassing.
Shirone and Amy turned around simultaneously.
A boy with messy hair was approaching with a sneer.
Behind him stood a tall, curly-haired young man and a heavily made-up girl, standing arrogantly as if guarding the rear.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Long time no see, Amy.
When Jokre extended his hand, Amy stood still, lost in thought.
Since entering magic school, she hadn't known anyone outside of her school friends.
Especially not any boys.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Um, sorry... but who are you?
Jokre's face twisted.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: How could she not remember him?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Even if she had rejected him, being confessed to should be an unforgettable memory.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: At least, that's what Jokre thought.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: He had never been confessed to by a girl, but if it ever happened, he was confident it would be a lifelong bragging point.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Do you not know, or are you pretending not to? Jokre. Alpheas Magic School. You really don't remember?
Amy mumbled.
Amy's eyes widened as she pointed at Jokre.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Jokre? Jokre...
Though she didn't seem genuinely pleased, her exaggerated surprise made Jokre puff up with pride in front of his friends.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Hmph, shouldn't you greet me properly?
Huh? What greeting?
Jokre swept his arm over his friends behind him and pointed his thumb at himself.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: I hear you're still in school, but we're official mages. That means we're unofficial 10th-grade mages.
Official mage.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy's heart fluttered.
Since entering Alpheas Magic School at twelve, it was a term that had never left her mind.
Praying her face wasn't stiff, Amy extended her hand to Jokre.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I see. Congratulations. You finally graduated.
At the time, Amy was thirteen.
She barely remembered Jokre, but she clearly recalled looking down on him when he transferred as if fleeing.
A smile spread across Jokre's face.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Amy's poker face was perfect, but he could see her heart without her opening up.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: How frustrated must she be, watching him from the position of a genius from the Karmis family who had failed her graduation exam?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Hmph, how does it feel? When you rejected me, it was much more humiliating than this.
Jokre turned his gaze to Shirone, who was standing idly.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Seeing a famous person here. Arian Shirone. The kingdom's top prospect, right?
Shirone remained silent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He could tell from the tone that Jokre wasn't approaching with friendly intentions.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Want some advice from a senior? A prospect is just a prospect. The real competition starts when you become an official mage. Well, for a commoner like you, you're doing well. Haha!
Amy shouted angrily.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Jokre! What are you saying? Apologize to Shirone right now!
Why should I? I'm not wrong. Oh, was that a public secret? But it's better for your mental health to give up. It's already been published in the journal. Every student knows.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Really? Then should I say whatever comes to mind too? Are you okay with that?
Jokre's eyes narrowed.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: He didn't know what she was going to say, but he couldn't help feeling pricked.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: After five years, Amy had grown into a much prettier girl than she was as a child.
Her hair was permed, something he hadn't seen even in the journal, and she wore bright, cute clothes.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: It was clear she had dressed up with care.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Tch! I guess this look is only for Shirone?
Amy was the girl most coveted by the boys at Eins Magic School.
Across the kingdom, the number would be much higher.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Thinking that she dressed up and put on makeup just for one person made him feel like he had lost to Shirone without even fighting.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: I'm disappointed, Amy. How can you date a commoner unless you're out of your mind? No matter how skilled he is, he's still just a student.
Seeing Shirone frown without retorting after being insulted as a commoner only made Jokre more annoyed.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: He should at least argue, but he doesn't say a word? Is he ignoring me? No, he must be scared of the term 'official mage.' Pathetic, hiding behind a girl... tsk tsk.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Jokre was mistaken.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To Shirone, who had even fought against Bosun, a certified 4th-grade mage, the title of official mage wasn't much of a threat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The reason Shirone was frowning wasn't Jokre but the Armand he was wearing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A person's mind flows like water.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And Armand's precise control was a delicate function that synchronized almost 100% with its owner's mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he couldn't perfectly control his emotions, Armand would move before he could calm himself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's why it was called a cursed sword.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, it wouldn't have mattered to its previous owner, Zion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He could just kill as his emotions dictated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Shirone had to continue his school life normally.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As a commoner with no one to rely on, committing murder would put him in an irreversible situation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The problem was that after returning from Kazra, it was harder to control his emotions than before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Though the demon Behemoth was trapped in the sealing formation, it was connected to Shirone's mind through the keyword 'anger.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If his emotions amplified to an uncontrollable state, Armand would decapitate his opponent without a second thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I guess I shouldn't have worn it. I'll have to be more careful in the future.
Seeing Shirone unable to retaliate, Jokre clicked his tongue and pointed to the game area, speaking to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: It seems you can't believe everything in the journal. You couldn't even win a doll for your girlfriend. Mind if we show you how it's done?
Amy shrugged.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's a game anyone can play if they pay. Go ahead if you want.
Jokre's group entered the game area.
Vivian, seemingly aware of Shirone's challenge, spoke loudly on purpose.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Guys, if you win the Ice Queen doll, you have to give it to me.
The announcer handed over the box of snowballs.
Although Jokre paid, it was Rudvans who received the box.
Jokre looked back at Shirone and Amy, pointing his thumb at Rudvans.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Rudvans is our school's 'Tuku' representative player. You can expect great things.
Tuku is a combat sport derived from ancient warfare, where teams of five compete in an arena with obstacles, trying to hit opponents with rubber balls.
While amateurs, including children, can enjoy it, at the semi-professional level of school competitions, the speed of fastballs can reach up to 160 kilometers per hour.
Additionally, tactics using three-cushion shots and various types of breaking balls have been developed, making it an exciting yet injury-prone sport.
Rudvans was the ace of his school, and the Eins Tuku team was strong enough to reach the quarterfinals in last year's national competition.

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: Well... let's start with a trial shot.
Rudvans' stance was clearly different from that of an ordinary person.
Each movement had a mechanism, and they connected smoothly to create the best throwing power.
As he swung his arm forward, the snowball flew with a whoosh.
It hit the target precisely, and Vivian cheered.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Great! A success on the first try! As expected of our school's ace!
